summary:
The hypoglossal nerve is unique as it exclusively serves motor control without providing sensory input to the palate; other nerves contribute either directly or indirectly by serving adjacent regions such as the mouth, ear area, and throat. Thus,
\[ D \] stands out uniquely here since no part of this nerve transmits tactile senses specifically related to the palate itself.
